details: The agent ran appropriate kubectl commands against the cluster (get all, describe deployment, describe pod, get events, describe replicaset, rollout history) and produced a correct, complete diagnosis. It correctly identified: (1) the rollout is stuck with Progressing=False and ProgressDeadlineExceeded, (2) the root cause is the invalid image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist causing ImagePullBackOff on the v2 pod, and (3) the old v1 pods (nginx:1.27-alpine) are still serving traffic because the rolling update cannot proceed. All findings are directly supported by the command outputs observed.
